title: Satire Meets Slop: The Onion's Bold Play for Control of Alex Jones's Infowars
content:
Satirical website The Onion's fight to gain control of Infowars may have noble intentions, but it faces challenges in the age of disinformation. Alex Jones seems content in his new home, where his loyal base is greeted with scattered layouts and questionable pop-up ads. Meanwhile, not a single dollar has been paid to the Sandy Hook families. Newsweek reached out to Jones for comment, but received no response.

In a time of rampant AI slop and widespread misinformation, everyone is susceptible to bots and online vitriol. Media entrepreneur Steven Rosenbaum noted that readers often struggle to differentiate between news, parody, opinion, and fact. He admits to questioning the authenticity of almost every video he sees online. When all information becomes untrustworthy, people may begin to assume everything is a lie, threatening democratic functions. Rosenbaum warns that a satirical version of Infowars might just be a bridge too far.

The Onion announced its plans to give any profits directly to victims of the school shooting. In a message to readers, The Onion's fictional owner said the website will become a place where panic and capital feed on each other. This dark humor was paired with fake ads and a video featuring a comedian doing an exaggerated impression of Jones.

When Stephen Colbert launched his outlandish Republican pundit character years ago, it was funny and refreshing. Looking back, that level of polarization in the news seems quaint compared to today's climate. Satire has always been a tool to cope with political absurdity, but the current reality often outpaces the jokes.

Ultimately, some critics argue that no amount of money raised from a satirical Infowars makes up for the ongoing damage. Even if the Sandy Hook families get every penny they are owed, Jones's harmful rhetoric continues elsewhere. We can chuckle at a funny headline, but the sobering reality remains that voices like his are still streaming live.
